sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content:
Systemic therapy for CNS tumors includes alkylating agents, targeted therapies, immunotherapies, and intrathecal treatments. Blood-brain barrier penetration is a key consideration. Treatment is often based on histology and molecular markers [BRAIN-D, GLIO-A].

By stage and setting:
- Adjuvant treatment, WHO grade 2 oligodendroglioma (IDH-mutant, 1p19q codeleted), KPS ≥60: Preferred: RT + adjuvant PCV (category 1). Alternatives: RT + adjuvant temozolomide; RT + concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide. Biomarker requirements: IDH-mutant, 1p19q codeleted.
- Adjuvant treatment, glioblastoma, IDH-wild-type, KPS ≥60, age ≤70: Preferred: RT + concurrent temozolomide and adjuvant temozolomide + TTF (category 1). Alternatives: RT + concurrent and adjuvant temozolomide. Biomarker requirements: None.
- Recurrent or progressive glioblastoma, KPS ≥60: Preferred: Bevacizumab; Temozolomide (if prior long interval); Lomustine; Carmustine; PCV; IDH inhibitor (if IDH-mutant). Alternatives: Regorafenib; Combination therapy with bevacizumab. Biomarker requirements: MGMT methylation may predict response to alkylating agents.
- Relapsed or refractory primary CNS lymphoma: Preferred: High-dose methotrexate rechallenge (if prior long response); Other systemic therapies (ibrutinib, rituximab, temozolomide, lenalidomide, pomalidomide); High-dose chemotherapy with stem cell rescue (category 2B). Alternatives: Tisagenlecleucel; Axicabtagene ciloleucel. Biomarker requirements: None.
- Brain metastases from NSCLC with EGFR mutation: Preferred: Osimertinib; Lazertinib + Amivantamab-vmjw (for exon 19 del/L858R). Alternatives: Other EGFR-TKIs (erlotinib, afatinib); Chemotherapy combinations. Biomarker requirements: EGFR sensitizing mutation.
- Brain metastases from melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation: Preferred: Dabrafenib/Trametinib; Cobimetinib/Vemurafenib. Alternatives: Immunotherapy (ipilimumab/nivolumab). Biomarker requirements: BRAF V600E mutation.
- Progressive vestibular schwannoma in NF2-SWN: Preferred: Bevacizumab (preferred); Brigatinib. Alternatives: Everolimus; Lapatinib. Biomarker requirements: NF2 germline mutation.

cards.name: PCV (procarbazine, lomustine, vincristine)
cards.detail: Setting: Adjuvant for grade 2/3 oligodendroglioma. Cycle days: 28. Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, nausea, neuropathy.

cards.name: Temozolomide adjuvant (Stupp regimen)
cards.detail: Setting: Glioblastoma adjuvant. Cycle days: 28 (5/28 schedule). Key toxicities: Lymphopenia, fatigue, nausea, myelosuppression.

cards.name: Bevacizumab
cards.detail: Setting: Recurrent glioblastoma, brain metastases, NF2-SWN. Cycle days: 28. Key toxicities: Hypertension, proteinuria, hemorrhage, thromboembolism.

cards.name: High-dose methotrexate for PCNSL
cards.detail: Setting: Induction for PCNSL. Cycle days: 14-21. Key toxicities: Nephrotoxicity, mucositis, myelosuppression.

cards.name: Vorasidenib
cards.detail: Setting: IDH-mutant glioma (grade 2) with residual/recurrent disease. Cycle days: Continuous. Key toxicities: ALT elevation, fatigue, headache.

timeline.title: 1st Line (Adjuvant)
timeline.body: RT + adjuvant PCV (for oligodendroglioma); RT + concurrent temozolomide and adjuvant temozolomide + TTF (for glioblastoma)

timeline.title: 2nd Line (Recurrent/Progressive)
timeline.body: Bevacizumab; Temozolomide; Lomustine; Carmustine; PCV; IDH inhibitor (if IDH-mutant); Regorafenib; Bevacizumab combinations

timeline.title: 3rd Line (Relapsed/Refractory PCNSL)
timeline.body: High-dose methotrexate rechallenge; Ibrutinib; Rituximab; Temozolomide; Lenalidomide; Pomalidomide; High-dose chemotherapy with stem cell rescue; Tisagenlecleucel; Axicabtagene ciloleucel

timeline.title: 4th Line (Brain metastases)
timeline.body: Osimertinib/Lazertinib+Amivantamab (NSCLC EGFR+); Dabrafenib/Trametinib or Cobimetinib/Vemurafenib (melanoma BRAF V600E+)
